paper.title: Identifying Sagittarius Stream Stars By Their APOGEE Chemical Abundance Signatures

extraction.summary: One hypervelocity star candidate identified from chemical clustering and orbit integration. Its APOGEE ID is not available in the provided source files (full Table 1 is external); no identifiers could be extracted. Extraction is partial pending external data.

method_chain.summary: APOGEE DR14 data including stellar parameters, chemical abundances, and radial velocities for ~176,000 red giants. Also the Sgr core sample from Hasselquist+2017 containing 158 stars with S/N>70.

source_refs.context: APOGEE DR14 data description

method_chain.summary: Apply quality cuts to select Milky Way stars for clustering: 3550 < Teff < 4200 K, S/N > 70, no ASPCAPBAD flag, [Fe/H] > -1.2.

source_refs.context: definition of sample with cuts

method_chain.summary: k-means clustering in 6-dimensional chemical space ([O/Fe], [(C+N)/Fe], [Mg/Fe], [Al/Fe], [Mn/Fe], [Ni/Fe]) with k=16, tuned to recover Sgr core and kinematic control sample. Identifies 68 chemical Sgr stream candidates.

source_refs.context: k-means clustering tuning and selection of k=16

method_chain.summary: Match chemical candidates to Gaia DR2 using TOPCAT nearest neighbor within 1 arcsec. 62 of 68 have Gaia proper motions.

source_refs.context: Gaia cross-match description

method_chain.summary: Adopt spectro-photometric distances from APOGEE DR14 value-added catalog (Hayden+2014 method), accurate to ~20%.

source_refs.context: distances from DR14 VAC
APOGEE radial velocities

method_chain.summary: Assume Solar radius R0 = 8.0 kpc, circular velocity v_circ = 220 km/s, and solar motion from Schönrich+2010 (exact values not reported).

source_refs.context: assume circular velocity 220 km/s at R=8.0 kpc
circular velocity 220 km/s

parameters.name: solar_motion_u
parameters.description: U component of solar peculiar motion not explicitly stated

source_refs.context: Solar motion as measured by Schönrich2010
Solar motion as measured by Schönrich2010
Solar motion as measured by Schönrich2010
circular velocity 220 km/s at 8.0 kpc; solar motion from Schönrich+2010

method_chain.summary: Convert observed positions, proper motions, distances, and radial velocities to Galactocentric UVW and total Galactocentric space velocity using left-handed Galactic Cartesian system.

source_refs.context: calculation of UVW velocities and orbit integration

method_chain.summary: Adopt MWPotential2014 from galpy (spheroidal bulge, Miyamoto-Nagai disk, NFW halo).

source_refs.context: MWPotential2014 used for integration
MWPotential2014 as default potential in galpy

method_chain.summary: Integrate orbits forward and backward for 1.5 Gyr in MWPotential2014 using galpy. Also integrate in Law & Majewski 2010 potential for comparison.

source_refs.context: integrate orbit forward and backward 1.5 Gyr

method_chain.summary: Determine if star is bound or unbound based on orbit integration. The HVS is found to be unbound in MWPotential2014, reaching >700 kpc and no bound orbit.

source_refs.context: HVS is completely unbound from the Milky Way

method_chain.summary: Discuss possible origins of HVS, ruling out black hole ejection and suggesting dynamical interactions or systematic proper motion errors.

source_refs.context: possible origins of the HVS candidate

candidate_groups_considered.description: 68 stars selected by k-means clustering as Sgr chemical candidates, with 62 having Gaia proper motions. The paper classifies them into Sgr stream (35), accreted halo (20), bulge (4), HVS (1), and others (2).
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Only the HVS candidate is potentially unbound; the rest are bound to the Milky Way. The HVS identifier is not available in the provided source files (only top of Table 1 shown; full catalog external).

source_refs.context: orbit group classification including HVS
Table 1 header and note indicating full list external

candidate_groups_considered.description: The hypervelocity star candidate identified as unbound from the Milky Way, with total Galactocentric velocity 577 km/s. Its APOGEE ID is not provided in the submitted manuscript (only in the external full table).
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Paper clearly states it is unbound and discusses possible origins, but the identifier cannot be extracted from the available files.

source_refs.context: description of HVS candidate and its unbound status
